steps.title: Map the spoken-print contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts a sermon, which is only heard and depends on memory, with a printed book, which remains as a record. The printed book can be returned to, cited, and preserved for ages, so it outlasts spoken delivery.

steps.title: Reject reversals and tangents
steps.content: Choice B reverses the passage by making spoken messages more memorable than printed ones. Choice C introduces expertise, which is not discussed. Choice D shifts focus to whether authors are remembered, while the passage focuses on the endurance of printed words. The answer is A.
